description_long: This triumphal arch features a semi-circular arrangement of multiple evenly spaced columns supporting a series of rounded arches. The columns are slender and topped with simple capitals, while the arches above are constructed from a reddish-brown material, likely brick or terracotta, contrasting with the pale stone of the columns. The structure is open, allowing passage through each archway, and lacks any solid walls or roof. There are no visible decorative reliefs, sculptures, or inscription panels, giving the arch a minimalist and utilitarian appearance. The overall design is reminiscent of Roman architectural styles, emphasizing symmetry and repetition. The arch would serve as a decorative or commemorative element in an urban park, plaza, or along a boulevard.
description: A semi-circular triumphal arch with multiple pale stone columns supporting reddish-brown rounded arches, minimalist and open in design.
description_view_0: The arch is seen from an angle, highlighting the depth and curvature of the structure, with columns and arches clearly visible.
description_view_1: A direct view of the arch's open side, showing the full semi-circular arrangement and the even spacing of the columns.
description_view_2: The opposite angle to view 0, emphasizing the curvature and the alignment of the columns and arches.
description_view_3: A rear view of the arch, displaying the back curvature and the uniformity of the columns and arches.
